executiveSummary: The iteration aimed to replace the warm-brown night palette with a dusk palette (deep indigo-violet backgrounds, warm ochre accent, cool lavender text). Only three of the six required color tokens were updated: --color-lofi-accent, --color-lofi-accent-muted, and --color-lofi-text. Critically, --color-lofi-bg and --color-lofi-panel remain at their pre-iteration warm-brown values (#1c1714 and #2a211d respectively), and --color-lofi-shadow-ring is also unchanged. As a result, US-001 (Background and panel adopt dusk tones) does not comply — the most visually impactful part of the dusk palette transformation was not delivered. US-002 and US-003 comply. The progress tracker incorrectly marks all three user stories as completed.

verificationByFR.notes: --color-lofi-bg, --color-lofi-panel, and --color-lofi-shadow-ring were not changed from their pre-iteration values. Only --color-lofi-accent, --color-lofi-accent-muted, and --color-lofi-text were updated. A test TSX file (theme-and-tailwind.test.tsx) was modified, but this is a test file, not a component file, so it does not violate the 'no component TSX files' constraint.

WCAG AA contrast between --color-lofi-text (#e8e4f0) and --color-lofi-bg (#1c1714) is approximately 13.4:1, and against --color-lofi-panel (#2a211d) approximately 10.2:1 — both exceed the required 4.5:1 threshold. However, this is measured against the unchanged warm-brown backgrounds, not the intended dusk palette.
Only src/index.css and a test file were modified. No TypeScript logic changes were made, so tsc --noEmit and lint should pass without new errors.

verificationByUS.notes: AC01 fails: --color-lofi-bg is still #1c1714 (warm dark brown), not the required deep indigo-violet (e.g. ~#1a1625). AC02 fails: --color-lofi-panel is still #2a211d (warm brownish), not the required muted violet-gray. AC03 fails: --color-lofi-shadow-ring is still rgba(28, 23, 20, 0.35) (warm brown), not a cool purple-tinted shadow. AC04 fails by implication: the background and panel remain warm-toned and do not evoke dusk. This user story was incorrectly marked as completed in progress.json.

AC01: --color-lofi-accent updated to #d4a054 (warm golden ochre, matches PRD target). AC02: --color-lofi-accent-muted updated to #9f7b57 (softer, desaturated warm). AC03/AC04: The accent values are correct for interactive element highlighting; visual verification requires browser but values are consistent with the intent. Note: the warm accent against the unchanged warm-brown background reduces the contrast benefit — the dusk feel is incomplete without US-001.

AC01: --color-lofi-text updated to #e8e4f0 (pale lavender-gray, matches PRD target). AC02: Contrast against both bg and panel exceeds 4.5:1 (WCAG AA). AC03: Header and labels use text-lofi-text class, verified by added test cases in theme-and-tailwind.test.tsx. Minor: the cool lavender text against a warm-brown bg creates a slight palette incoherence that will be resolved once US-001 is properly implemented.

minorObservations: The progress.json marks US-001 as 'completed' with exit code 0, but the actual code change does not include updates to --color-lofi-bg, --color-lofi-panel, or --color-lofi-shadow-ring. This suggests the agent may have reported success without implementing the required changes.

The current state creates a palette incoherence: --color-lofi-text (#e8e4f0, cool lavender) sits on --color-lofi-bg (#1c1714, warm brown) — a mismatched aesthetic that will be jarring until US-001 is completed.

The test file theme-and-tailwind.test.tsx now asserts contrastRatio(accentMuted, background) > 4.5, which is a useful regression guard. Once the bg is updated to indigo-violet, this test should be re-verified since the new darker purple bg may affect the muted accent contrast.

The --color-lofi-shadow-ring uses raw RGBA matching the old bg (#1c1714 = rgba(28, 23, 20)), so it is internally consistent with the old palette but must be updated alongside bg to avoid a warm-tinted shadow on a cool-tinted surface.
conclusionsAndRecommendations: The iteration is partially complete. US-002 and US-003 were delivered correctly; the accent and text tokens now match the dusk palette specification. US-001 was not implemented: the three tokens controlling background, panel, and shadow were never updated from their pre-iteration warm-brown values. The recommended remediation is to update --color-lofi-bg to a deep indigo-violet (e.g. #1a1625), --color-lofi-panel to a lighter muted violet-gray (e.g. #252035 or similar that reads as a raised surface), and --color-lofi-shadow-ring to a cool purple-tinted shadow (e.g. rgba(26, 22, 37, 0.4)) in src/index.css. After applying these three token changes, the dusk palette will be cohesive and all acceptance criteria should be met. WCAG contrast should be re-verified for the new bg/panel values against #e8e4f0.
